name: Spaghetti w/ Eggplant Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Pare the eggplant and cut into 3/4-inch cubes. Place the eggplant cubes in a colander; sprinkle with salt and toss. Let stand and drain in the sink or over a bowl, tossing occasionally for about 1 hour.
2. Meanwhile, chop the undrained tomatoes using scissors or a knife; reserve.
3. Pit the olives. Rinse the anchovies; pat dry. Chop the olives, anchovies, and capers together; reserve.
4. Rinse the eggplant and drain well; squeeze out excess moisture with a clean kitchen towel.
5. Core, seed, and chop the green bell pepper. Chop the onion; mince the garlic.
6. Heat the olive oil in a 3-quart non-corrosive saucepan over medium-high heat. Add the eggplant, salted pepper, and onion; sauté for 5 minutes.
7. Add the minced garlic; cook for an additional 30 seconds.
8. Stir in the reserved tomatoes and their liquid, basil, and red pepper sauce. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat and simmer covered for about 20 minutes.
9. Uncover and continue cooking, stirring occasionally, until the sauce is thickened to a coating consistency, about 10 more minutes.
10. Stir in the reserved olive mixture and parsley. Simmer covered for an additional 5 minutes.
11. Just before serving, cook the spaghetti in a large pot of boiling salted water just until al dente, about 8-12 minutes. Drain well.
12. Toss the cooked spaghetti with the sauce in a heated serving bowl. Serve with grated Parmesan or Romano cheese for sprinkling. Makes 4-6 servings.
